title: Roasted Red Onions with White Beans & Fried Breadcrumbs
summary: Hands down one of my favourite ways to cook with red onions. Roasting the sliced onions hard until they colour with natural sugars, then hitting the softened onions straight out of the oven with plenty of red wine vinegar is a winning way to cook them.

ingredients.label: 2-3 Tbsp Red Wine Vinegar, plus extra
ingredients.name: Red Wine Vinegar

ingredients.label: 700g Jar White Beans (such as cannellini), or use canned
ingredients.name: Drained Cooked Cannellini Beans

ingredients.label: 1 Red Chilli, more or less to taste
ingredients.name: Red Chilli

ingredients.label: 8 Anchovy Fillets, more or less to taste
ingredients.name: Anchovy

ingredients.label: Small Bunch Fresh Oregano or Marjoram
ingredients.name: Fresh Oregano

method.description: Heat the oven to 170°C and line a large baking tray with baking parchment.
Peel the onions and slice into 1cm-thick rings. Arrange the onions in a single layer on the baking tray, then add 2/5 of your olive oil to coat, plus a good pinch of salt and plenty of black pepper.

Bake for 30 mins until softened and bronzed, removing from the oven after 15 mins to stir through the dried oregano (flip the onions over at this stage). If you want yet more colour, grill under a high heat for 5-10 mins to speed the process up. Remove from the oven and immediately splash all over with the vinegar.
Meanwhile, cook the breadcrumbs in ⅖ of your olive oil in a large frying pan over a medium heat for 3-4 mins until toasted and golden brown. Set aside on a plate lined with kitchen paper.
Drain the beans, then finely chop the parsley, chilli and anchovies. Stir all three through the beans, plus the remainder of your olive oil.
Add the onions and beans to a large serving plate, adding extra vinegar if you like. Roughly chop the oregano and sprinkle all over, adding a generous spoon of fried breadcrumbs to serve.

chefs.name: Claire Thomson
chefs.summary: Claire Thomson (@5oclockapron) is a chef, author of 10 cookbooks and mum of 3 daughters. Claire began cooking professionally before smartphones were invented. Her food is delicious as it is uncomplicated, she is almost always in an apron cooking breakfast, lunch or dinner. In short, there is nothing Claire can’t cook.
